reviews.review_msg: the prof himself is entertaining and he cares about his students and our learning, but the TA is such a tough grader and bascom is totally fine with their grading style. like yeah you'll enjoy the class and have some fun but the bad grade kinda turns things around. but tbh still a good class, just dont be dumb like me
Bascom is a great prof. He cares abou students. He is very approachable and we can learn a lot from him. He also has a good sense of humour. I would take his class again.

the prof himself is entertaining and he cares about his students and our learning, but the TA is such a tough grader and bascom is totally fine with their grading style. like yeah you'll enjoy the class and have some fun but the bad grade kinda turns things around. but tbh still a good class, just dont be dumb like me
Bascom is a great prof. He cares abou students. He is very approachable and we can learn a lot from him. He also has a good sense of humour. I would take his class again.

the prof himself is entertaining and he cares about his students and our learning, but the TA is such a tough grader and bascom is totally fine with their grading style. like yeah you'll enjoy the class and have some fun but the bad grade kinda turns things around. but tbh still a good class, just dont be dumb like me
Bascom is a great prof. He cares abou students. He is very approachable and we can learn a lot from him. He also has a good sense of humour. I would take his class again.
hard but fair - funny and well . . . . kind
intimidating, wasted a lot of time talking about class/group conduct etc

I had been learning a lot about conducting ethical research (e.g., seeking consent from research participants) in his awesome course! I even received a certificate for conducting ethical research on people called the TCPS 2: Core 2022!
Only a couple of assignments. TA helped a lot. The prof lectures were confusing sometimes but the canvas posts explained everything pretty well.
Lectures were tedious but the tutorials were fun at times and helpful. All the feedback for the assignments was  super helpful and caring for the other assignments.

Nick's lectures could use some work but the assignments are arranged for you to learn a lot if you pay attention to his feedback, (which is very thorough, more than I have ever had before). He is not an easy marker but if you work hard you will become a better researcher. Good class, I feel like I learned more than I expected.

The other reviews are accurate regarding his lectures. He kinda rambles on and is unclear what he is trying to say. I get lost in his lectures a lot and often find them unhelpful. The good thing is that attendance isn't mandatory so you can skip his classes since attending his class is honestly kinda a waste of time.
he was so unclear and his lectures unhelpful to the assignments

This class was very difficult to keep up with. It was online, but required a lot of collaboration with other students. Weekly modules were frequently late, assignments were unclear. Felt like a lot of busy work for no real benefit. Way more work than a 200-level course should be -- I'm a 4th year and this was my heaviest course this semester.

Very caring and passionate about the material, but lectures are difficult to keep up with. Often wanders a bit and isn't entirely clear about the points he's making. Assignments are a little difficult to understand, but the grading checklists are helpful for figuring out what components need to be in them.
Very passionate, just not good at teaching the content. He is unclear about what he actually wants with most assignments and rubrics/descriptions are very vague. The grading is also very tough.
I agree with the other posts, but really just talk to him. He's a nice guy he'll work with you if you have questions or concerns. super passionate about the subject too which is a plus!

Despite being passionate about the topics, he lacked the ability to clearly teach the class. His grading was all over the place and the syllabus didn't explain how anything would be marked. And, so much was marked incredibly unfairly without reason -- Avoid this professor!

just terrible. he didn't give criteria for participation and then added it as an assignment after the semester had finished. knocked me down by 20% despite the fact that I had participated extensively during the semester. Just terribly disorganized professor and TA. do NOT enroll with dr. scott unless you're willing to destroy your GPA.

confused, and cuts you off when you ask questions for clarification. Dr. Scott seems like a kind person, but as a professor... Such simple course content was made so much more difficult than need be especially for a first year course. Beware of rubrics! they're not exactly what he's looking for and you won't know what until the end of the semester

Lots of assignments. Here's what you need to know: read the criteria like crazy + ask a lot of questions, he doesn't make everything clear + follow the structures of other research works or articles. Read his work He is a nice guy but very laid back and unstructured. Grading will vary and he has harder/higher standards than he seems to have.

I understood course content completely, followed instructions, and received horrifically low grades. Students were being docked marks over page limit confusions and then marks were restored later? Class avg. on assignments were in the 60s and 70s until "reviewed." All students shared the same frustrations, and yet our feedback was ignored.

The class content is quite complex but manageable if you have taken courses about qualitative and quantitative research before, All semester the prof was very unclear on assignment descriptions/grading criteria and loved to talk about his own research (bicycles) a lot.

Dr. Scott's lecture is good, however where he falls short is his assignments. The assignments are worth a lot but it is unclear what is expected, and he is a tough grader. I feel that I have a good grasp on the course content but it is not at all reflected in my grade, the assignments are that hard. Start them early so you can ask him things early.

Easiest quantitative credit! If you have a difficult time with math then I recommend taking this class because it's a mix of both qualitative and quantitative work. The tutorials helped me a lot. The TA was really nice and easy to talk to. The prof is quite helpful but really disorganized with his assignments and emails.

The professor for this course is not punctual whatsoever. Showed up late almost every lecture, but more importantly handed marked things back really late, posted grades late, and waited to the last day to give back final things, participation and final grades. Replies to emails very late. Very disorganized/flustered, not very professional.

This professor and class is a joke. Super disorganized, late marker, few things that are weighted heavy. Seems easy but marks ridiculously. DO NOT TAKE THIS WITH THIS PROF. Got straight As in all my other 3 classes, this one was far from an A when I worked hard, did what was asked. Prof had a different opinion on my project and graded me very poor

Would have dropped this if I didn't need a quantitative. Hands back assignments really late. TA this semester does not know English and his feedback is not helpful at all if you want to improve. Better to go to the prof directly for help. Tough grader. The prof replied back to my email 1 day before the assignment was due. Take this class if needed

This is the worst class (besides statistics). The lectures and tutorials are such a bore, the marking is really tough, only talks about and focuses on what the prof is interested in, unorganized, 2 big assignments at the start of the semester. Such a waste of my time. Not impressed, if it wasnt mandatory Id drop right away. Learned nothing.

So far this class is going pretty well. Prof makes his syllabus throughout the semester. Nicholas is very passionate and understanding. The TA this semester is really useless and not that helpful. Tutorials are pointless unless you have Nicholas as the one giving lecture in tutorial. The assignments are pretty straightforward but a lot of work.

The prof is young and laidback. Tough marker! Does not reply to emails on time. Gives back assignments really late. Very unorganized, the TA this semester does not seem to be too familiar with the Canadian education system so hopefully that does not affect my grade.

Nick is super high-energy and really loves what he teaches. Unfortunately he is sometimes tactless and doesn't think before he speaks. If you make an effort to speak up in class and offer your ideas he will love you. Beware: he talks about his research a LOT and he makes up assignment guidelines as he goes. However, I learned a lot in his course.

Professor Scott is really engaging and passionate about this course, it shows in his lectures. I can see why people think SA255 is hard because it requires for individualized thinking. Overall great structure and prof for a mandatory Sociology major course. Attend classes and get a higher mark!

It's the basics of social research, not a terribly exciting topic, so it might be a bit unfair to blame the prof for the class not being very interesting. Lots of busy work in class. Expectations are clear for each of the writing assignments. This isn't a hard class or a hard prof. If you're struggling, make time to talk to him.

Nicholas is kind and passionate about his work but is very vague on what he wants for his assignments. His TA was the worst, she would show up late and often would not be engaged in the lecture. He doesn't take attendance during tutorials so I'm not sure how he based our participation marks on.

Nice person and is clearly passionate but what he is teaching. BUT it is clearly is own self interests that he is teaching and not teaching the student learn foundations of social research. Assignments are extremely vague and only shows examples what he want for assignments from student work AFTER THE ASSIGNMENT IS DUE!

Nick's an engaging prof. Made lecture and tutorials engaging with different activities. It is a very hands on learning environment, which I enjoyed. (but takes lots of time) The one thing I found challenging was how vague the assignments were. However, he does give lots of good and helpful feedback.

Nick is a nice teacher however he is not the best professor. He tends to fall off track often. The assignments we have to do are ridiculous. No one understands what we are supposed to do/ achieve. He is very vague about his expectations and does not clearly explain any concepts

Nick is  clearly passionate about what he's teaching. However, his approach to this course is what I would expect from an advanced 3 or 400 level applied research methods course. The way he teaches this course is not appropriate for the level most students are at and does not allow students to establish a solid knowledge base for research methods.
Assignments are vague and confusing
I enjoyed the way he teaches because it was more hands on and therefore I felt I got a better understanding of the concepts. His instructions aren't clear.

You're forced to go and do fieldwork in some random place in Vancouver- really don't have time for that. On my days off, I am not interested in going somewhere that takes a relatively long time to get to, especially when it's raining to do work (which the prof does not provide much guidance on). He is passionate about what he teaches though.

As a fairly new professor, his marking is hard. Considering 70% of your overall grade are from assignments, he does not provide very detailed instructions as they aren't very organized and are not very clear; they are very broad and vague. He expects your analysis to be just as strong as his, and if not, it will show in the low mark he gives you.

Thought this class was  an intro to social research but it ended up being about Vancouver neighborhoods, sustainability and mobility. He seemed to expect the class to understand these concepts without teaching the foundations and research methods. Assignment heavy with little criteria and notice. Replies to emails take a long time.

SA255 was a waste of time. Poorly organized: did not upload slides after saying he would and being asked repeatedelywould upload project details 10 days before due date vague assingment details. Irrelevant lectures that dont relate to assingments.  Emphasis on mobile methods and city neighbourhoods yet this is a Quatitative course.  DO NOT TAKE!!
His classes are confusing. Jumps from one topic to another not explaining either one correctly.

Really passionate professor, so lectures are interesting. Taking notes is a waste of time. The exams are easy if you read carefully and study the material - slides are all posted online. Returns assignments with a huge amount of helpful comments. Friendly, but can be intimidating - takes teaching very seriously.
Good prof

He is very opinionated about research stuff and owns his own research firm. He's talkative and amusing to listen to and easy to understand. He was helpful in his tutorials if you asked for help but tutorials seemed like a lecture. The course though is kinda hard. Midterm was hard, final was easier. Overall, I enjoyed being in his class.

a horrible man! i saw him make fun of student's "dumb" questions and poor test marks. is available for extra help but isn't very helpful. unfortunately this class is a req for soc major but if you can avoid taking it with him do it!

Chris was always available for extra help and wants his students to succeed. His standards are high and forces you to think through many details, particularly for research proposals, but the skills gained from that are invaluable.

One of the best profs I've ever had; she genuinely wants you to succeed in her class and will help you as much as she can. Final assignment isn't real easy, but you start it at the beginning of term and work on it as class goes on. Great class if taught by her. NO FINAL!

Cathy is great! She makes a boring subject interesting. Posts her PP notes online so you can go over them before/after class. Lots of class participation/ presentations but she makes you feel at ease from the 1st class. Very approachable and helpful, I would recommend her although the final assignment is HARD (lit review&research proposal!)
Great professor. Very nice person and very helpful. Highly recommended.
I thought it was a good class...
Excellent
Terrific class... reading load is a bit heavy at times but the articles are interesting.  take his class!
great prof!

His use of a caim voice is a facade used for conformity. This is a self righteous jerk, same thing for the TAs he selects. an intellectual know it all. its not inspiring work. and the class is training and not an education or freedom of thought. It Makes me regret transferring to SFU. This was Not a useful class.

His lecture notes are completely useless. His amount of criteria needed to explain his assignments are so brief that I practically lost marks on areas that I didn't know I would've lost. I followed what the criteria asked me to do but he still deducted my marks on many areas. What a jerk...Also, his TA's are pathetic too. Not helpful and mean!!!

Very anal in marking!!  His marking scale was unfair because he nitpicks every single tiny detail while his TAs mark so much easier.  He also requires you to think outside the box by A WHOLE LOT, normally in research proposals, you follow all the criteria/guidelines provided. However, you are required to think of every single tiny detail outside th

The course material was all over the place during the semester, poor coordinated. The text book was dry and hard to read, and there was a lot of reading assigned. He went off topic A LOT and talked about his own research and that of his wife. He was very reluctant in helping me and asked i speak with the TA's instead. Personally, I couldn't underst
The guy knows his stuff! awesome!

He teaches what can only be referred to as a gentleman's class. This quality is so hard to find sometimes, as it requires a genuine interest in the subject and a wish for students to succeed by the teacher.
Quiet, but friendly. Available for help outside of classes, and gives useful advice. Managed to make boring subject matter somewhat entertaining.
He's so cute, I just want to pinch him! He is smart and a great prof. Very organized and supplies helpful outlines and notes for each class via WebCT. Awesome!! Take his class!

I would voluntarily take this class again with Fernando as the instructor (as long as it wasn't at 8:30 am again). He is very organized and assignments really help to solidify material covered in lecture. Highly recommended. Best instructor I have had at SFU.
He's great!  I love his teaching style.  He was my prof and TA.  Marking was fair.  What I like best about him is that he actually CARES about his students!  I absolutely recommend him!
No Comments

This was my first class with Chris and I thought it was fantastic. He is very knowledgeable of the course content and I really liked how he was able to link the work we were doing with opportunities outside of continuing within Academia. Chris provided a lot of feedback on assignments and was very approachable. Fabulous learning experience.

I had high hopes for Chris, considering this class covers such dry material and I was hoping he could make it better. However, he is quite full of himself and talks about his accomplishments more than necessary. He is also a difficult marker and not the friendliest in emails. Only take this class if it is required for your major.

Cool guy - very funny. I had him as my TA too and tutorials were so useless. His lectures were useful and he is very knowledgeable. I wouldn't say that he is a hard marker. He's a very fair marker that gives a lot of feedback. If you make an impression on him he'll remember your name. Get read for a lot of work!

Chris is a very good teacher. Kind of arrogant, the occasional bizarre joke but who cares? That just makes the interesting. He's a hard marker but he gives so much feedback that your grades shouldn't suffer so long as you heed his advice. Tests are easy! Would take another class with him in a flash!

I read some of the less flattering comments on here before I took the course but I have to say, I actually enjoyed the way Chris lectured and looked forward in coming to class. His sarcasm and sense of humor is my kind of stuff. This guy also provides tons of feedback because he wants you to do well. Big plus that he also understands technology.
Best prof! Heavy course load but very informative and interesting.

Yes, he has a somewhat abrasive, irreverent personality. However, he is also quite funny. I generally can't stand arrogant people, so I was wary of taking this class with him after reading these comments, but I greatly enjoyed the experience. He provides tons of feedback on assignments, and he is quite easy to talk to in his office hours.

Chris shows that he is very passionate about students bettering social research skills. He gives detailed feedback on assignments. He is also very approachable during office hrs. More importantly, his lectures & text don't sugar coat the realities of obstacles you may encounter in research.

Class is valuable, so is the textbook. Teacher on the other hand ... in love with himself. Employs "edgy"  teaching style (with lectures full of "edgy" naughty words, punctuated by "ughs" and "ahhs") devoid of substance. Got good grade in class, yet still got strange email deriding my efforts. Strange stuff. Strange teacher. Highly over-rated.

Probably one of the best profs I have ever had. Yes the class is hard, yes you have to do TONS of work...but it definately was the most informative, interesting class I've ever taken. Make use of his office hours, he is EXTREMELY helpful and wants you to do well. If you cant deal with dry sarcasm though, stay away. But overall, hes an amazing prof

This class was extremely valuable for anyone interested in research. Chris covers a lot of ground, and keeps the class engaged in lectures. He actually asks his students more questions than we ask him, which helps in retaining information. On the downside, this course requires A LOT of work, do not take it in a heavy courseload.
no comment

Difficult class. I would recommend going to lecture and reading the text. There are a lot of terms to remember for the midterm which was overwelming but by the end of the course the terms were easier to understand and the final was easier b/c of the assignments. Don't let his first lecture scare you, he seems less "standoffish" later on.
The most useful class I have taken so far. Hella lot of work but not overly hard. Great instructor, very passionate about research and teaching. Lectures were worth going to.

I have never had a prof that provided as much constructive feedback on my work as this guy. He seems passionate about what he teaches and he really seems to care about his students. THIS CLASS IS HARD but the lectures are informative and entertaining and the support that the prof provides is outstanding if you take advantage of it.
interesting lectures. funny and opinionated dude. hard marker. fast response time to emails, but doesn't give straight answers. definitely not an easy class.

A really helpful prof and in general I did really like his class. While I did appreciate how much he disliked big, pretentious words, I did find his constantly referring to himself as an anarchist to be a bit much. All in all though a tough and worthwhile course!

The concepts discussed in class are very clearly presented both in lecture and the textbook. The assignments were so useful for completing the final project. Not a huge fan of multiple choice exams, but not bad. Chris is approachable and kind. Takes a real interest in the work students are doing, if they exhibit they are interested in the class.

Cuts thru the **** quickly, very techno-saavy, puts course notes on-line after each class, assignments submitted on-line, evaluations of assignments delivered on-line.  All-in-all, more prof's could/should copy his teaching methods.  He is very good at using the computer in various supportive capacities.  Would take a course from him again.
Hands down, the best. Tough, fair and funny
No Comments
No Comments

Great Prof.  Friendly and helpful if you take advantage of office hours and email.  Tons of experience and knows his stuff.  He is also young and in touch with the role of technology in social research.  Be warned, 255 is a heavy course with a huge project (20-50pg) and lots of assingments that take a lot of effort.  Hard work pays off though!

At first, I found him to be a very engaging and passionate speaker. But as the course went on, I found him to be quite arrogant and repetitive. To his credit, he is knowledgeable and has plenty of experience. He is a 'to the point' kind of guy which comes off as blunt and insensitive.

Great Prof! Basically, what you work for is what you get. This course definately requires a lot of work but not hard to do good in. Exams are fair. Very passionate about his work. Makes a dull course interesting. Gives out a lot of feedback. If you ask for help he will give it.

GREAT prof.  Good sense of humor, great dedication, and totally fair.  Chris makes serious efforts to help and be available to students.  Gotta do the work cause the course isn't easy but Chris makes the dry content seem interesting...love the rants!

gives heavy workload BUT is very helpful at the same time! Shows a an incredible amount of passion in the course and TRULY cares about his students....his sarcastic humor adds much flavor to the boring-nature class:)

this course will be hard no matter WHO teaches it cuz it IS a research methods course. so material=HARD. but CHRIS= AWESOME PROF!! i love his little jokes inbetween lectures and he's so organized and helpfull. chris is the ideal prof:clear & not only helpfull, but ALWAYS available! he's also cute ;)
I was dreading taking this (required) class but Chris' passion makes it interesting. It is quite challenging but he and his TAs are VERY approachable.
No Comments

There are no surprises. he's clear, explains exactly what is expected in each assigment or exam, always willing to answer questions, and highly recommends that you do (if you want to succeed)! and although its a req'd course that seems dry, chris makes it tolerable.
